title: tilia platyphyllos subsp. caucasica
intro: Tilia platyphyllos subsp. caucasica, also known as the Caucasian large-leaved linden, is a subspecies of the Tilia platyphyllos tree, native to the Caucasus region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, headaches, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Tilia platyphyllos subsp. caucasica include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and other female health issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are popular in traditional medicine, as well as being used in aromatherapy and perfumery.
